title: Thrust: Adaptively Propels Large Language Models with External Knowledge

structured.summary: The paper introduces IAPEK, an instance-level adaptive retrieval framework that decides when to augment pretrained language models with external knowledge based on a novel metric called Thrust, which estimates the model's internal knowledge sufficiency. Extensive experiments across multiple tasks demonstrate that Thrust-guided selective retrieval improves accuracy and efficiency compared to naive all-instance retrieval.

strengths.evidence: IAPEK selectively retrieves external knowledge only when necessary, improving efficiency and reducing noise.

strengths.point: Thrust metric effectively estimates knowledge sufficiency
strengths.evidence: Thrust score distributions correlate well with task knowledge requirements and guide retrieval decisions that yield performance gains.
Evaluated on 7 classification and 5 open-domain QA datasets, showing consistent accuracy improvements and cost savings.
Demonstrates that zero-shot PTLMs often do not benefit from external knowledge, while fine-tuned models do, highlighting realistic deployment considerations.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient comparison with prior adaptive retrieval or uncertainty-based methods
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not adequately position Thrust relative to existing confidence or uncertainty-based gating techniques.

weaknesses.point: Lack of detailed methodology on clustering and distance metrics
weaknesses.evidence: Missing specifics on clustering algorithm, initialization, hyperparameter sensitivity, and ablations on distance metric choice.

weaknesses.point: Limited evaluation of retrieval effectiveness and efficiency metrics
weaknesses.evidence: No precision/recall of retrieved knowledge, latency, or end-to-end cost metrics reported despite motivation on efficiency.
Requires ~200 task-specific examples for clustering, limiting zero-shot use; also less clear performance on generation tasks without clear clusters.

weaknesses.point: No statistical significance testing or confidence intervals
weaknesses.evidence: Reported accuracy improvements lack formal significance validation.

weaknesses.point: Ambiguity in thrust score computation and threshold selection
weaknesses.evidence: Mathematical formulation is abstract with no pseudocode; threshold λ tuning details are missing.

questions.question: How does Thrust compare quantitatively to uncertainty-based or confidence-based retrieval gating approaches?
questions.motivation: Clarifying novelty and empirical advantages over prior adaptive retrieval methods is important for assessing contribution.

questions.question: Can the method be extended to work effectively in true zero-shot scenarios without task-specific labeled samples for clustering?
questions.motivation: Addressing cold start limitations would enhance applicability to many real-world tasks.

critique_points.point_001: Insufficient comparison with prior adaptive retrieval or uncertainty-based methods. The paper does not adequately position Thrust relative to existing confidence or uncertainty-based gating techniques
critique_points.point_002: Lack of detailed methodology on clustering and distance metrics. Missing specifics on clustering algorithm, initialization, hyperparameter sensitivity, and ablations on distance metric choice
critique_points.point_003: Limited evaluation of retrieval effectiveness and efficiency metrics. No precision/recall of retrieved knowledge, latency, or end-to-end cost metrics reported despite motivation on efficiency
critique_points.point_004: Restricted generalizability and applicability. Requires ~200 task-specific examples for clustering, limiting zero-shot use; also less clear performance on generation tasks without clear clusters
critique_points.point_005: No statistical significance testing or confidence intervals. Reported accuracy improvements lack formal significance validation
critique_points.point_006: Ambiguity in thrust score computation and threshold selection. Mathematical formulation is abstract with no pseudocode; threshold λ tuning details are missing
